tasks.title: Implement Centralized Authentication and RBAC
tasks.description: Introduce a dedicated Identity Provider (IdP) and integrate JWT validation and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) across sensitive services. This will secure endpoints that currently lack identity and permission verification.
subtasks: Research and select an appropriate IdP (e.g., Keycloak, Auth0, Clerk).
Deploy and configure the chosen IdP.
Integrate JWT middleware validation into the Equipment Catalog Service (Task 2), RMS Service (Task 3), Finance Service (Task 4), and Customer Vetting Service (Task 5).
Define specific RBAC roles (e.g., admin, finance, sales, catalog_manager) and map them to service endpoints.
Implement authorization checks based on RBAC roles for all sensitive endpoints (e.g., POST /api/v1/catalog/products, GET /api/v1/payroll).
Update API documentation to reflect new authentication and authorization requirements.
acceptance_criteria: All sensitive API endpoints require a valid JWT for access.
Users with incorrect roles are denied access to restricted endpoints.
A dedicated IdP is deployed and managing user identities.
Successful authentication and authorization flows are demonstrated for each affected service.

tasks.title: Implement AI Agent Prompt Injection Safeguards
tasks.description: Address the prompt injection vulnerability in the Morgan AI Agent by implementing robust input sanitization, prompt hardening, and human-in-the-loop validation for sensitive actions.
subtasks: Implement an LLM security gateway or integrate prompt injection detection mechanisms (e.g., input sanitization, content filtering) for all inputs to the Morgan AI Agent.
Harden system prompts to explicitly define boundaries and prevent malicious instructions.
Require explicit human confirmation (via Signal/Chat) before the agent executes state-changing MCP tools like sigma1_create_invoice or sigma1_generate_quote.
Log all attempts at prompt injection and human confirmation requests.
acceptance_criteria: Attempts to inject malicious prompts are detected and blocked or mitigated.
The AI agent requests human confirmation before executing any destructive or sensitive MCP tool.
No unauthorized state changes can be triggered via prompt injection.

tasks.title: Implement Encryption at Rest for Sensitive Data
tasks.description: Implement encryption at rest for sensitive PII and financial data handled by the Finance and Vetting services, ensuring data stored in PostgreSQL is protected.
subtasks: Identify all sensitive fields in the Finance Service (Task 4) and Customer Vetting Service (Task 5) databases (e.g., payroll details, credit scores).
Implement application-level field encryption for these sensitive fields using a robust encryption library (e.g., aes-gcm in Rust).
Securely manage encryption keys, potentially integrating with a Key Management System (KMS) if available in the cloud environment.
Alternatively, investigate and configure database-level encryption at rest (TDE) for the sigma1-postgres cluster (Task 1) if application-level encryption is not feasible for all data.
Update database schemas and application code in Task 4 and Task 5 to handle encrypted data.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitive data fields in PostgreSQL are stored in an encrypted format.
Data can only be decrypted by authorized application components.
A data breach of the database would not immediately expose plaintext sensitive information.

tasks.title: Implement Security Audit Logging
tasks.description: Enhance observability by implementing structured security audit logging for critical actions across the RMS, Finance, and Vetting services. This ensures compliance and aids incident response.
subtasks: Define a standardized audit log format including actor ID, action, resource ID, timestamp, and outcome.
Integrate an audit logging library or mechanism into the RMS Service (Task 3), Finance Service (Task 4), and Customer Vetting Service (Task 5).
Instrument critical actions (e.g., CreateOpportunity, GetProject, POST /api/v1/invoices, GET /api/v1/payroll, POST /api/v1/vetting/run) to emit audit events.
Configure logs to be sent to a secure, append-only log destination (e.g., a dedicated logging sink, SIEM).
Ensure audit logs are immutable and tamper-proof.
Integrate with the existing OpenTelemetry/Jaeger setup for correlation, ensuring audit logs are distinct and secure.
acceptance_criteria: All specified critical actions generate structured audit logs.
Audit logs contain the required information (actor, action, resource, timestamp).
Audit logs are stored securely and are accessible for review.
No audit logs can be modified or deleted by unauthorized parties.

tasks.title: Enhance Kubernetes Secrets Management
tasks.description: Replace basic Kubernetes secrets with an external secrets management solution to improve security, enable automated rotation, and reduce the risk of credential exposure.
subtasks: Research and select an external secrets management solution (e.g., External Secrets Operator with AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault).
Deploy and configure the chosen secrets management solution within the Kubernetes cluster.
Migrate existing Kubernetes secrets (S3/R2 credentials, database connection strings) from native K8s secrets to the external solution.
Configure automated secret rotation for all managed credentials.
Update application deployments (Task 1 and others that consume secrets) to retrieve credentials from the new secrets management system.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitive credentials are no longer stored as native Kubernetes secrets.
Credentials are securely injected into pods via the external secrets management solution.
Automated secret rotation is configured and verified.
Applications successfully retrieve and use credentials from the new system.
